chunks.text: ORDER SHEET IN THE HIGH COURT OF SINDH AT KARACHI H.C.A. No.120 of 2024 M/s Abdul Sattar Arbani Builders & Co. Versus Province of Sindh & others Date Order with signature of Judge 1. For orders on office objection. 2. For orders on CMA 743/24 3. For hearing of main case 4. For orders on CMA 744/24 Dated: 08.04.2024 Malik Naeem Iqbal for appellant. Mr. Sandeep Malani, Assistant Advocate General. Mr. Ahmed Masood along with Mr. Shariq Razzak for respondent No.6. -.-.- Heard the counsel and perused record. Appellant has impugned an order of 15th March 2024 which is an ad-interim order. Malik Naeem Iqbal, learned counsel appearing for the appellant, submits that this order was obtained by concealment of facts and should not have been passed in presence of orders dated 23.11.2023 passed in High Court Appeals No.379 to 382 of 2023 and order passed in Suit No.330 of 2023 dated 04.10.2023. The order impugned in this appeal is only an ad-interim order and such defence and facts are yet to be appreciated by learned Single Judge whereas the appellant has directly jumped to avail the jurisdiction of this Court; they may be right in saying so but then they have to surrender themselves before the learned Single Judge for deciding the interim application expeditiously. We, therefore, deem it appropriate, in the light of the arguments of learned counsel for the appellant as it is claimed to be a covered in terms of above two orders, that the injunction application may be heard and decided preferably in three weeks’ time after notice to all concerned and hearing the parties/counsels. Appeal, along with listed applications, stands disposed of in the above terms.

We, therefore, deem it appropriate, in the light of the arguments of learned counsel for the appellant as it is claimed to be a covered in terms of above two orders, that the injunction application may be heard and decided preferably in three weeks’ time after notice to all concerned and hearing the parties/counsels. Appeal, along with listed applications, stands disposed of in the above terms. Judge Judge
